WITH OrderedContracts AS (
  SELECT 
    DriverId,
    ContractType,
    StartDate,
    EndDate,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY DriverId, ContractType ORDER BY StartDate) as rn
  FROM driverContracts
),
GroupedContracts AS (
  SELECT 
    oc1.DriverId,
    oc1.ContractType,
    oc1.StartDate,
    oc1.EndDate,
    oc1.rn,
    -- Check if there's a gap from the previous contract
    CASE 
      WHEN oc1.rn = 1 THEN 0
      WHEN oc1.StartDate <= DATE(oc2.EndDate, '+1 day') THEN 0
      ELSE 1
    END as IsNewGroup
  FROM OrderedContracts oc1
  LEFT JOIN OrderedContracts oc2 
    ON oc1.DriverId = oc2.DriverId 
    AND oc1.ContractType = oc2.ContractType 
    AND oc1.rn = oc2.rn + 1
),
GroupIdentifiers AS (
  SELECT 
    DriverId,
    ContractType,
    StartDate,
    EndDate,
    SUM(IsNewGroup) OVER (PARTITION BY DriverId, ContractType ORDER BY StartDate) as GroupId
  FROM GroupedContracts
)
SELECT 
  DriverId,
  ContractType,
  MIN(StartDate) AS StartDate,
  MAX(EndDate) AS EndDate
FROM GroupIdentifiers
GROUP BY DriverId, ContractType, GroupId
ORDER BY DriverId, ContractType, MIN(StartDate)

WITH RECURSIVE CTE_RN AS (
    SELECT 
        e.exp_id, 
        e.mid, 
        e.qty, 
        i.qty AS LimitQty, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY e.exp_id) AS rn 
    FROM Experiment AS e 
    INNER JOIN Inventory AS i ON e.mid = i.mid 
    WHERE e.mid = 2
), 
CTE_Recursive AS (
    SELECT 
        r.exp_id, 
        r.mid, 
        r.qty, 
        r.LimitQty, 
        r.rn, 
        CASE WHEN r.qty <= r.LimitQty THEN r.qty ELSE 0 END AS TotalConsumed,
        CASE WHEN r.qty <= r.LimitQty THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS Executable 
    FROM CTE_RN AS r 
    WHERE r.rn = 1 
    
    UNION ALL 
    
    SELECT 
        r.exp_id, 
        r.mid, 
        r.qty, 
        r.LimitQty, 
        r.rn, 
        rec.TotalConsumed + CASE WHEN r.qty <= (r.LimitQty - rec.TotalConsumed) THEN r.qty ELSE 0 END AS TotalConsumed,
        CASE WHEN r.qty <= (r.LimitQty - rec.TotalConsumed) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS Executable 
    FROM CTE_RN AS r 
    INNER JOIN CTE_Recursive AS rec ON rec.rn + 1 = r.rn
) 
SELECT 
    exp_id, 
    mid, 
    Executable 
FROM CTE_Recursive 
ORDER BY rn

WITH RECURSIVE vt AS (
    SELECT 
        t.*, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY CustomerID ORDER BY StartDate) AS seqnum 
    FROM transactions_1k AS t
), 
cte AS (
    -- Base case: first transaction for each customer (always unflagged)
    SELECT 
        vt.TransactionID, 
        vt.CustomerID, 
        vt.StartDate, 
        vt.EndDate,
        vt.EndDate AS last_unflagged_end_date,
        0 AS flag,
        vt.seqnum 
    FROM vt 
    WHERE seqnum = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: subsequent transactions
    SELECT 
        vt.TransactionID, 
        vt.CustomerID, 
        vt.StartDate, 
        vt.EndDate,
        CASE 
            -- If current transaction is more than 90 days after last unflagged end date, it becomes unflagged
            WHEN vt.StartDate > date(cte.last_unflagged_end_date, '+90 days') 
            THEN vt.EndDate
            -- Otherwise, keep the same reference end date
            ELSE cte.last_unflagged_end_date 
        END AS last_unflagged_end_date,
        CASE 
            -- Flag as 1 if within 90 days of last unflagged transaction's end date
            WHEN vt.StartDate <= date(cte.last_unflagged_end_date, '+90 days') 
            THEN 1
            -- Flag as 0 if more than 90 days after
            ELSE 0 
        END AS flag,
        vt.seqnum 
    FROM cte 
    JOIN vt 
        ON vt.seqnum = cte.seqnum + 1 
        AND vt.CustomerID = cte.CustomerID
) 
SELECT 
    cte.TransactionID, 
    cte.CustomerID, 
    cte.StartDate, 
    cte.EndDate,
    cte.flag
FROM cte 
ORDER BY cte.CustomerID, cte.StartDate

WITH RankedTrans AS (
  SELECT 
    trans_id,
    account_id,
    date,
    operation,
    k_symbol,
    bank,
    account,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY date) as rn
  FROM trans
),
FilledTrans AS (
  SELECT 
    t1.trans_id,
    t1.account_id,
    t1.date,
    COALESCE(t1.operation, 
      (SELECT t2.operation 
       FROM RankedTrans t2 
       WHERE t2.account_id = t1.account_id 
         AND t2.rn < t1.rn 
         AND t2.operation IS NOT NULL 
       ORDER BY t2.rn DESC 
       LIMIT 1)
    ) as operation,
    COALESCE(t1.k_symbol,
      (SELECT t2.k_symbol 
       FROM RankedTrans t2 
       WHERE t2.account_id = t1.account_id 
         AND t2.rn < t1.rn 
         AND t2.k_symbol IS NOT NULL 
       ORDER BY t2.rn DESC 
       LIMIT 1)
    ) as k_symbol,
    COALESCE(t1.bank,
      (SELECT t2.bank 
       FROM RankedTrans t2 
       WHERE t2.account_id = t1.account_id 
         AND t2.rn < t1.rn 
         AND t2.bank IS NOT NULL 
       ORDER BY t2.rn DESC 
       LIMIT 1)
    ) as bank,
    COALESCE(t1.account,
      (SELECT t2.account 
       FROM RankedTrans t2 
       WHERE t2.account_id = t1.account_id 
         AND t2.rn < t1.rn 
         AND t2.account IS NOT NULL 
       ORDER BY t2.rn DESC 
       LIMIT 1)
    ) as account
  FROM RankedTrans t1
)
UPDATE trans
SET 
  operation = (SELECT operation FROM FilledTrans WHERE FilledTrans.trans_id = trans.trans_id),
  k_symbol = (SELECT k_symbol FROM FilledTrans WHERE FilledTrans.trans_id = trans.trans_id),
  bank = (SELECT bank FROM FilledTrans WHERE FilledTrans.trans_id = trans.trans_id),
  account = (SELECT account FROM FilledTrans WHERE FilledTrans.trans_id = trans.trans_id)
WHERE trans_id IN (
  SELECT trans_id FROM trans 
  WHERE operation IS NULL OR k_symbol IS NULL OR bank IS NULL OR account IS NULL
)

WITH RankedTransactions AS (
    -- Get the last transaction at each gas station for each customer
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        transactionid,
        gasstationid,
        productid,
        amount,
        price,
        StartDate,
        transaction_time,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid 
            ORDER BY StartDate DESC, transaction_time DESC
        ) AS rn_per_station
    FROM transactions_1k
),
LastPerStation AS (
    -- Keep only the last transaction per gas station
    SELECT *
    FROM RankedTransactions
    WHERE rn_per_station = 1
),
CustomerIssues AS (
    -- Identify if customer has any amount < 10 and find the first occurrence
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        MIN(CASE 
            WHEN amount < 10 THEN StartDate || ' ' || transaction_time 
        END) AS first_issue_datetime,
        CASE 
            WHEN MIN(CASE WHEN amount < 10 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1 THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS has_issue
    FROM LastPerStation
    GROUP BY customerid
),
FirstIssueStation AS (
    -- Get the gas station of the first issue occurrence
    SELECT 
        lps.customerid,
        lps.gasstationid AS first_issue_gasstation
    FROM LastPerStation lps
    INNER JOIN CustomerIssues ci 
        ON lps.customerid = ci.customerid
    WHERE ci.has_issue = 1
        AND lps.amount < 10
        AND (lps.StartDate || ' ' || lps.transaction_time) = ci.first_issue_datetime
),
LastValidStation AS (
    -- Get the last gas station with amount >= 10 for customers without issues
    SELECT 
        lps.customerid,
        lps.gasstationid AS last_valid_gasstation
    FROM LastPerStation lps
    INNER JOIN CustomerIssues ci 
        ON lps.customerid = ci.customerid
    WHERE ci.has_issue = 0
        AND lps.amount >= 10
    ORDER BY lps.StartDate DESC, lps.transaction_time DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT 
    lps.customerid,
    lps.transactionid,
    lps.gasstationid,
    lps.amount,
    lps.price,
    lps.StartDate,
    lps.transaction_time
FROM LastPerStation lps
LEFT JOIN CustomerIssues ci ON lps.customerid = ci.customerid
LEFT JOIN FirstIssueStation fis ON lps.customerid = fis.customerid
LEFT JOIN LastValidStation lvs ON lps.customerid = lvs.customerid
WHERE 
    -- If customer has issues, show the first issue station
    (ci.has_issue = 1 AND lps.gasstationid = fis.first_issue_gasstation)
    OR
    -- If customer has no issues, show the last valid station
    (ci.has_issue = 0 AND lps.gasstationid = lvs.last_valid_gasstation)
ORDER BY lps.customerid, lps.StartDate, lps.transaction_time

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Get all refs for each username
user_refs AS (
    SELECT username, ref1 AS ref FROM users WHERE ref1 IS NOT NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT username, ref2 AS ref FROM users WHERE ref2 IS NOT NULL
),
-- Get all unique refs
all_refs AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ref FROM user_refs
    UNION
    SELECT DISTINCT owner FROM assets WHERE owner NOT IN (SELECT ref FROM user_refs)
),
-- Find connected components: refs that share a username
ref_connections AS (
    SELECT u1.ref AS ref1, u2.ref AS ref2
    FROM user_refs u1
    JOIN user_refs u2 ON u1.username = u2.username
    WHERE u1.ref < u2.ref
),
-- Recursively find all connected refs
connected_refs AS (
    SELECT ref AS ref, ref AS group_ref FROM all_refs
    UNION
    SELECT 
        CASE WHEN cr.ref1 < c.group_ref THEN cr.ref1 ELSE c.group_ref END,
        CASE WHEN cr.ref1 < c.group_ref THEN c.group_ref ELSE cr.ref1 END
    FROM connected_refs c
    JOIN ref_connections cr ON c.ref = cr.ref2
    UNION
    SELECT 
        CASE WHEN cr.ref2 < c.group_ref THEN cr.ref2 ELSE c.group_ref END,
        CASE WHEN cr.ref2 < c.group_ref THEN c.group_ref ELSE cr.ref2 END
    FROM connected_refs c
    JOIN ref_connections cr ON c.ref = cr.ref1
),
-- Get the minimum ref as the group identifier for each ref
ref_groups AS (
    SELECT ref, MIN(group_ref) AS group_id
    FROM connected_refs
    GROUP BY ref
),
-- Aggregate assets by group
result AS (
    SELECT 
        rg.group_id AS ref2,
        COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_count,
        GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_ids,
        GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT a.owner) AS owners
    FROM ref_groups rg
    LEFT JOIN assets a ON a.owner = rg.ref
    GROUP BY rg.group_id
)
SELECT 
    ref2,
    asset_count,
    asset_ids
FROM result
ORDER BY ref2

WITH ordered_changes AS (
  SELECT 
    entity_id,
    column_id,
    value,
    updated_at,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id, column_id ORDER BY updated_at) AS rn
  FROM player_stats_changes
),
all_timestamps AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT entity_id, updated_at
  FROM player_stats_changes
),
height_changes AS (
  SELECT 
    entity_id,
    value AS height,
    updated_at
  FROM ordered_changes
  WHERE column_id = 'height'
),
weight_changes AS (
  SELECT 
    entity_id,
    value AS weight,
    updated_at
  FROM ordered_changes
  WHERE column_id = 'weight'
)
SELECT 
  t.entity_id,
  (SELECT h.height 
   FROM height_changes h 
   WHERE h.entity_id = t.entity_id 
     AND h.updated_at <= t.updated_at 
   ORDER BY h.updated_at DESC 
   LIMIT 1) AS height,
  (SELECT w.weight 
   FROM weight_changes w 
   WHERE w.entity_id = t.entity_id 
     AND w.updated_at <= t.updated_at 
   ORDER BY w.updated_at DESC 
   LIMIT 1) AS weight,
  t.updated_at
FROM all_timestamps t
ORDER BY t.entity_id, t.updated_at

WITH qr1 AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup1 AS subgroup 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
), 
qr2 AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup2 AS subgroup 
    FROM superhero_group 
    WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
), 
combined AS (
    SELECT id, subgroup FROM qr1 
    UNION 
    SELECT id, subgroup FROM qr2
),
-- Find all pairs of IDs that share at least one subgroup
direct_connections AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT c1.id AS id1, c2.id AS id2 
    FROM combined c1 
    JOIN combined c2 ON c1.subgroup = c2.subgroup AND c1.id < c2.id
),
-- Build transitive closure using recursive CTE
connected_components AS (
    -- Base case: each ID connects to itself
    SELECT id AS id1, id AS id2
    FROM superhero_group
    
    UNION
    
    -- Direct connections (both directions)
    SELECT id1, id2 FROM direct_connections
    UNION
    SELECT id2 AS id1, id1 AS id2 FROM direct_connections
    
    UNION
    
    -- Transitive connections
    SELECT cc1.id1, cc2.id2
    FROM connected_components cc1
    JOIN connected_components cc2 ON cc1.id2 = cc2.id1
    WHERE cc1.id1 < cc2.id2
)
SELECT 
    sg.id,
    MIN(cc.id2) AS group_id
FROM superhero_group sg
LEFT JOIN connected_components cc ON sg.id = cc.id1
GROUP BY sg.id
ORDER BY sg.id

WITH r AS (

        utm_source,

        SUM(bounces) AS bounces
    FROM report 
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign, utm_source, utm_medium
)
SELECT 
    u.row_id,
    u.utm_campaign,
    u.utm_source,
    u.utm_medium,
    u.utm_content,
    u.utm_term,
    r.date_of_visit,
    r.sessions,
    r.pageviews,
    r.bounces
FROM utm AS u
LEFT JOIN r 
    ON u.row_id = r.id 
    AND (u.utm_campaign = r.utm_campaign OR (u.utm_campaign IS NULL AND r.utm_campaign IS NULL))
    AND (u.utm_source = r.utm_source OR (u.utm_source IS NULL AND r.utm_source IS NULL))

WITH race_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        gender_id,
        race_id,
        COUNT(*) AS cnt
    FROM superhero
    WHERE gender_id IS NOT NULL AND race_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY gender_id, race_id
),
ranked_races AS (
    SELECT 
        gender_id,
        race_id,
        cnt,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY gender_id ORDER BY cnt DESC) AS rank_desc,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY gender_id ORDER BY cnt ASC) AS rank_asc
    FROM race_counts
)
SELECT 
    gender_id,
    race_id,
    cnt,
    CASE 
        WHEN rank_desc <= 5 THEN 'Top 5'
        WHEN rank_asc <= 5 THEN 'Bottom 5'
    END AS category,
    rank_desc,
    rank_asc
FROM ranked_races
WHERE rank_desc <= 5 OR rank_asc <= 5
ORDER BY gender_id, rank_desc

WITH attendance_count AS (

    FROM attendance 
    GROUP BY link_to_event
),
new_attendance_count AS (
    SELECT link_to_event, COUNT(link_to_member) AS count 
    FROM new_attendance 
    GROUP BY link_to_event
)
SELECT 
    COALESCE(a.link_to_event, n.link_to_event) AS link_to_event,
    COALESCE(a.count, 0) AS attendance_count,
    COALESCE(n.count, 0) AS new_attendance_count
FROM attendance_count a
FULL OUTER JOIN new_attendance_count n ON a.link_to_event = n.link_to_event
WHERE COALESCE(a.count, 0) <> COALESCE(n.count, 0)

WITH cte AS (
    SELECT 
        pv1.session_id, 
        pv1.post_id, 
        pv1.ts AS start_time, 
        MIN(pv2.ts) AS end_time,
        MAX(pv2.view_perc) AS view_perc
    FROM view_logs AS pv1 
    JOIN view_logs AS pv2 
        ON pv1.session_id = pv2.session_id 
        AND pv1.post_id = pv2.post_id 
        AND pv1.event_name = 'view_start'
        AND pv2.event_name = 'view_end'
        AND pv1.ts < pv2.ts 
    GROUP BY pv1.session_id, pv1.post_id, pv1.ts
) 
SELECT 
    session_id, 
    post_id, 
    start_time, 
    end_time,
    view_perc,
    CASE 
        WHEN (end_time - start_time > 3 AND view_perc > 0.8) THEN 'yes' 
        ELSE 'no' 
    END AS has_meaningful_view 
FROM cte

WITH numbered AS (
    SELECT 
        id, 
        str, 
        val, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) as rn
    FROM example
),
grouped AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        str,
        val,
        rn,
        SUM(CASE WHEN val IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
            OVER (ORDER BY id ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS grp
    FROM numbered
),
filled AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        str,
        val,
        FIRST_VALUE(val) OVER (PARTITION BY grp ORDER BY id) AS valx
    FROM grouped
)
SELECT 
    id, 
    str, 
    val, 
    valx 
FROM filled
ORDER BY id

SELECT 
    SUM(CASE WHEN DATE(timestamp) = DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day') THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS yesterday_revenue,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN DATE(timestamp) = DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day') THEN amount ELSE 0 END) - 
         SUM(CASE WHEN DATE(timestamp) = DATE('2022-01-27', '-8 days') THEN amount ELSE 0 END)) / 
        CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN DATE(timestamp) = DATE('2022-01-27', '-8 days') THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS REAL),
        2
    ) AS pct_change_week_ago,
    SUM(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y-%m', timestamp) = strftime('%Y-%m', DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day')) THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS mtd,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y-%m', timestamp) = strftime('%Y-%m', DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day')) THEN amount ELSE 0 END) - 
         SUM(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y-%m', timestamp) = strftime('%Y-%m', DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day', '-1 month')) 
                   AND CAST(strftime('%d', timestamp) AS INTEGER) <= CAST(strftime('%d', DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day')) AS INTEGER) 
                   THEN amount ELSE 0 END)) / 
        CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN strftime('%Y-%m', timestamp) = strftime('%Y-%m', DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day', '-1 month')) 
                      AND CAST(strftime('%d', timestamp) AS INTEGER) <= CAST(strftime('%d', DATE('2022-01-27', '-1 day')) AS INTEGER) 
                      THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS REAL),
        2
    ) AS pct_change_month_prior
FROM transactions
